# openclaw-skills

![Language](https://img.shields.io/badge/language-Go-00ADD8?logo=go&logoColor=white)
![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT--0-green)
![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-OpenClaw-blueviolet)

Custom skills for [OpenClaw](https://openclaw.ai) agents, published to the [Claw Skills](https://clawskills.sh) registry.

## Skills

| Skill | Description | Status |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| [google-workspace](./google-workspace/) | Gmail, Calendar, Contacts, Drive (with comments), Docs, and Sheets | Published (`panthrocorp-google-workspace`) |
| [zoho-mail](./zoho-mail/) | Full read/write Zoho Mail access (EU data centre) | Published (`panthrocorp-zoho-mail`) |

## Installation

Install any skill via clawhub:

```bash
clawhub install panthrocorp-google-workspace
clawhub install panthrocorp-zoho-mail
```

## Security

Every skill in this repository is designed with a hostile-instance threat model in mind:

- OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with HKDF-SHA256 key derivation. No unencrypted fallback exists.
- Gmail and Contacts access is strictly read-only with no write code paths.
- Calendar, Drive (comments), Docs, and Sheets write operations are opt-in and gated at both config and runtime level.
- Zoho Mail provides full read/write access, scoped to a dedicated agent mailbox.
- Google Cloud and Zoho API projects are scoped to only the required APIs.

See each skill's README and [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for the full security policy.

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for guidelines on branching, commits, and adding new skills.

## Licence

This repository is released under the [MIT No Attribution](./LICENSE) licence.

content.text:
All tasks complete. Here's a summary of what was created and modified:

**New files** (`aws-s3/`):
- `package.json` with `@openclaw/aws-s3` scoped package, esbuild + `@aws-sdk/client-s3` as devDeps
- `src/index.mjs` barrel re-export of `@aws-sdk/client-s3`
- `esbuild.config.mjs` bundling to CJS, Node 22 target
- `test/exports.test.mjs` using `node:test` with `createRequire` (CJS interop)
- `SKILL.md` with `requires.npm` metadata
- `CLAUDE.md`, `LICENSE`, `.gitignore`
- `package-lock.json` (generated)

**Modified files**:
- `.release-please-config.json` added `aws-s3` with `release-type: "node"`
- `.release-please-manifest.json` added `"aws-s3": "0.1.0"`
- `.github/dependabot.yml` added npm ecosystem entry
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` added `build-npm` job, updated `gate` to depend on both
- `.github/workflows/release-please.yml` added outputs + `publish-aws-s3` job
- `CLAUDE.md` split "Adding a new skill" into Go and npm subsections
- `README.md` added Node.js badge, aws-s3 to skills table, install command, security note

Build + test verified locally: 4/4 tests pass, `npm pack` produces a 246 kB tarball with the correct 4 files.

Want me to run the review agents before committing, or shall we proceed?

input.description: Get full diff for review agents

content.content:
diff --git a/.github/dependabot.yml b/.github/dependabot.yml
index 41cd834..a04ad6d 100644
--- a/.github/dependabot.yml
+++ b/.github/dependabot.yml
@@ -21,6 +21,16 @@ updates:
       - dependencies
     open-pull-requests-limit: 5
 
+  - package-ecosystem: npm
+    directory: /aws-s3
+    schedule:
+      interval: weekly
+    commit-message:
+      prefix: "fix"
+    labels:
+      - dependencies
+    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
+
   - package-ecosystem: github-actions
     directory: /
     schedule:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 52ac273..c4e34a1 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -55,16 +55,62 @@ jobs:
         if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
         run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
 
+  build-npm:
+    name: Build & Test (npm)
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    defaults:
+      run:
+        working-directory: aws-s3
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+
+      - name: Check for skill changes
+        id: changes
+        run: |
+          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
+          PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
+          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
+            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          else
+            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+          fi
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Build
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm run build
+
+      - name: Test
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm test
+
+      - name: Pack (dry-run)
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
+        run: npm pack --dry-run
+
+      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
+        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
+        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
+
   gate:
     name: gate
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    needs: [build]
+    needs: [build, build-npm]
     if: always()
     steps:
-      - name: Check build result
+      - name: Check build results
         run: |
-          if [ "${{ needs.build.result }}" = "failure" ] || [ "${{ needs.build.result }}" = "cancelled" ]; then
-            echo "Build failed or was cancelled"
-            exit 1
-          fi
+          for result in "${{ needs.build.result }}" "${{ needs.build-npm.result }}"; do
+            if [ "$result" = "failure" ] || [ "$result" = "cancelled" ]; then
+              echo "A build failed or was cancelled"
+              exit 1
+            fi
+          done
           echo "All checks passed"
diff --git a/.github/workflows/release-please.yml b/.github/workflows/release-please.yml
index 6671ab9..fa527ed 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/release-please.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/release-please.yml
@@ -20,6 +20,8 @@ jobs:
       gw_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--tag_name'] }}
       zm_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--release_created'] }}
       zm_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--tag_name'] }}
+      s3_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--release_created'] }}
+      s3_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--tag_name'] }}
     steps:
       - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
         id: release
@@ -136,3 +138,50 @@ jobs:
         env:
           CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
           VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
+
+  publish-aws-s3:
+    name: Publish aws-s3
+    needs: release-please
+    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    defaults:
+      run:
+        working-directory: aws-s3
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+        with:
+          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
+
+      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: "22"
+
+      - name: Install dependencies
+        run: npm ci
+
+      - name: Test before release
+        run: npm run build && npm test
+
+      - name: Build and pack
+        id: pack
+        run: |
+          npm run build
+          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
+          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
+        run: |
+          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
+          gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
+        env:
+          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+
+      - name: Publish to clawhub
+        if: success()
+        run: |
+          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
+          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
+          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
+          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
+        env:
+          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
diff --git a/.release-please-config.json b/.release-please-config.json
index 7c79aee..198da5b 100644
--- a/.release-please-config.json
+++ b/.release-please-config.json
@@ -24,6 +24,18 @@
           "jsonpath": "$.version"
         }
       ]
+    },
+    "aws-s3": {
+      "release-type": "node",
+      "component": "aws-s3",
+      "changelog-path": "CHANGELOG.md",
+      "extra-files": [
+        {
+          "type": "yaml",
+          "path": "SKILL.md",
+          "jsonpath": "$.version"
+        }
+      ]
     }
   },
   "initial-version": "0.1.0",
diff --git a/.release-please-manifest.json b/.release-please-manifest.json
index d3fab1c..13098f9 100644
--- a/.release-please-manifest.json
+++ b/.release-please-manifest.json
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 {
   "google-workspace": "0.5.0",
-  "zoho-mail": "0.3.0"
+  "zoho-mail": "0.3.0",
+  "aws-s3": "0.1.0"
 }
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index 4a31c42..d5edcc3 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -84,12 +84,23 @@ Release-please bot PRs must be merged manually. GitHub does not trigger `pull_re
 
 ## Adding a new skill
 
+### Go-based skills
+
 1. Create a new directory at the repo root with its own `go.mod`, `main.go`, `cmd/`, and `internal/` packages.
 2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `google-workspace/SKILL.md` for the schema).
 3. Add a `.goreleaser.yml` for cross-compilation.
 4. Include an MIT-0 `LICENSE` file (clawhub requirement).
 5. Add a `gomod` entry for the new skill directory in `.github/dependabot.yml`.
-6. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` and `.release-please-manifest.json`.
+6. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` (with `release-type: "go"`) and `.release-please-manifest.json`.
+
+### npm-based skills
+
+1. Create a new directory at the repo root with `package.json`, source files, and an esbuild config.
+2. Add a `SKILL.md` with YAML frontmatter (see `aws-s3/SKILL.md` for the schema). Use `requires.npm` instead of `requires.bins`.
+3. Include an MIT-0 `LICENSE` file (clawhub requirement).
+4. Add an `npm` entry for the new skill directory in `.github/dependabot.yml`.
+5. Add the new skill path to `.release-please-config.json` (with `release-type: "node"`) and `.release-please-manifest.json`.
+6. Add a `build-npm` style CI job and a publish job in the release workflow.
 
 ## Git conventions

=== NEW FILE: aws-s3/CLAUDE.md ===
# aws-s3

## Overview

Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents. Unlike the Go-based skills in this
repo, this is an npm package that bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive
dependencies into a single CommonJS file using esbuild.

## Architecture

- `src/index.mjs` barrel re-exports everything from `@aws-sdk/client-s3`
- `esbuild.config.mjs` bundles `src/index.mjs` into `dist/index.cjs` (CommonJS, Node 22 target)
- `dist/index.cjs` (built, gitignored) is the complete bundled SDK
- `test/exports.test.mjs` validates the bundle exports expected symbols using `node:test`

## Build

```bash
npm ci && npm run build
```

## Test

```bash
npm test
```

Tests require a prior build as they validate the `dist/index.cjs` output.

## How it works

The gateway container has Node.js but no internet and no `@aws-sdk` installed. This package
bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and all its transitive dependencies into a single CJS file
using esbuild. The tarball from `npm pack` is installed into the container via
`npm install <tarball>`.

## Credentials

Uses the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. On EC2/ECS this resolves via IMDS
automatically. No environment variables are required for authentication. The IMDS hop
limit must be 2+ when running inside Docker (handled by PAN-52).

## Release

Release-please uses `release-type: "node"` to version this package. On release, `npm pack`
produces a tarball that is uploaded to the GitHub release and published to clawhub.

---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@openclaw/aws-s3"
    emoji: "🪣"
    homepage: https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---

# AWS S3 Skill

Pre-bundled AWS S3 SDK for use inside the OpenClaw gateway container. All transitive
dependencies are bundled. No internet access required at install time.

## Installation

Install from the release tarball into the gateway container:

```bash
TAG=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases" \
  | grep -o '"tag_name":"aws-s3/v[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
VERSION=${TAG#aws-s3/v}
URL="https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases/download/${TAG}/openclaw-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install "$URL" --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
```

Or from a downloaded tarball:

```bash
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install /path/to/openclaw-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz \
  --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
```

## Usage

```js
const { S3Client, ListObjectsV2Command, GetObjectCommand } = require("@openclaw/aws-s3");

const client = new S3Client({});

const list = await client.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({
  Bucket: process.env.EMAIL_BUCKET_NAME,
  Prefix: `parsed/${agentName}/`,
}));

const obj = await client.send(new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: process.env.EMAIL_BUCKET_NAME,
  Key: "parsed/agent/email.json",
}));
const body = await obj.Body.transformToString();
```

## Authentication

Credentials resolve via the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. In an EC2/ECS
environment this uses IMDS (Instance Metadata Service) automatically. Ensure the IMDS
hop limit is set to 2 or higher when running inside Docker.

No environment variables are required for authentication. `AWS_REGION` should be set
if the region cannot be inferred from instance metadata.

## Important

- This package bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive dependencies.
- No network access is needed at install time.

import { createRequire } from "node:module";

const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
const mod = require("../dist/index.cjs");

  it("exports S3Client as a constructor", () => {
    assert.equal(typeof mod.S3Client, "function");
  });

  it("exports ListObjectsV2Command", () => {
    assert.equal(typeof mod.ListObjectsV2Command, "function");
  });

  it("exports GetObjectCommand", () => {
    assert.equal(typeof mod.GetObjectCommand, "function");
  });

  it("can instantiate S3Client without network calls", () => {
    const client = new mod.S3Client({ region: "us-east-1" });
    assert.ok(client);
    client.destroy();
  });
});

=== NEW FILE: aws-s3/CLAUDE.md ===
# aws-s3

## Overview

Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents. Unlike the Go-based skills in this
repo, this is an npm package that bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive
dependencies into a single CommonJS file using esbuild.

## Architecture

- `src/index.mjs` barrel re-exports everything from `@aws-sdk/client-s3`
- `esbuild.config.mjs` bundles `src/index.mjs` into `dist/index.cjs` (CommonJS, Node 22 target)
- `dist/index.cjs` (built, gitignored) is the complete bundled SDK
- `test/exports.test.mjs` validates the bundle exports expected symbols using `node:test`

## Build

```bash
npm ci && npm run build
```

## Test

```bash
npm test
```

Tests require a prior build as they validate the `dist/index.cjs` output.

## How it works

The gateway container has Node.js but no internet and no `@aws-sdk` installed. This package
bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and all its transitive dependencies into a single CJS file
using esbuild. The tarball from `npm pack` is installed into the container via
`npm install <tarball>`.

## Credentials

Uses the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. On EC2/ECS this resolves via IMDS
automatically. No environment variables are required for authentication. The IMDS hop
limit must be 2+ when running inside Docker (handled by PAN-52).

## Release

Release-please uses `release-type: "node"` to version this package. On release, `npm pack`
produces a tarball that is uploaded to the GitHub release and published to clawhub.

---
name: AWS S3
description: Self-contained AWS S3 SDK bundle for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      npm:
        - "@openclaw/aws-s3"
    emoji: "🪣"
    homepage: https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---

# AWS S3 Skill

Pre-bundled AWS S3 SDK for use inside the OpenClaw gateway container. All transitive
dependencies are bundled. No internet access required at install time.

## Installation

Install from the release tarball into the gateway container:

```bash
TAG=$(curl -fsSL "https://api.github.com/repos/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases" \
  | grep -o '"tag_name":"aws-s3/v[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
VERSION=${TAG#aws-s3/v}
URL="https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills/releases/download/${TAG}/openclaw-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install "$URL" --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
```

Or from a downloaded tarball:

```bash
docker exec openclaw-gateway npm install /path/to/openclaw-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz \
  --prefix /home/node/.openclaw/bin/.npm-global
```

## Usage

```js
const { S3Client, ListObjectsV2Command, GetObjectCommand } = require("@openclaw/aws-s3");

const client = new S3Client({});

const list = await client.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({
  Bucket: process.env.EMAIL_BUCKET_NAME,
  Prefix: `parsed/${agentName}/`,
}));

const obj = await client.send(new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: process.env.EMAIL_BUCKET_NAME,
  Key: "parsed/agent/email.json",
}));
const body = await obj.Body.transformToString();
```

## Authentication

Credentials resolve via the AWS SDK default credential provider chain. In an EC2/ECS
environment this uses IMDS (Instance Metadata Service) automatically. Ensure the IMDS
hop limit is set to 2 or higher when running inside Docker.

No environment variables are required for authentication. `AWS_REGION` should be set
if the region cannot be inferred from instance metadata.

## Important

- This package bundles `@aws-sdk/client-s3` with all transitive dependencies.
- No network access is needed at install time.
- The gateway container must have Node.js available.
- All S3 commands from the SDK are available, not just the ones shown above.

Review the following changes for security issues. This is a new npm package skill (aws-s3) being added to the openclaw-skills repository. It bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 into a self-contained CJS file for use inside a Node.js gateway container that has no internet access.

Focus on:
- Supply chain risks (npm dependencies, esbuild bundling)
- CI/CD pipeline security (GitHub Actions workflows, secret handling)
- Credential handling (AWS SDK default provider chain via IMDS)
- Package integrity (what ships in the tarball)
- Any injection vectors in the workflow YAML

CHANGED FILES (modified):
- .github/dependabot.yml: Added npm ecosystem entry for /aws-s3
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: Added build-npm job with change detection, updated gate job
- .github/workflows/release-please.yml: Added s3 outputs and publish-aws-s3 job
- .release-please-config.json: Added aws-s3 package with release-type: "node"
- .release-please-manifest.json: Added "aws-s3": "0.1.0"

NEW FILES:

- aws-s3/src/index.mjs: `export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";`
- aws-s3/esbuild.config.mjs: Bundles to dist/index.cjs, platform: "node", target: "node22", format: "cjs"
- aws-s3/test/exports.test.mjs: Tests using node:test runner with createRequire
- aws-s3/SKILL.md: Clawhub manifest with requires.npm metadata
- aws-s3/CLAUDE.md: Agent context
- aws-s3/LICENSE: MIT-0
- aws-s3/.gitignore: node_modules/, dist/

CI workflow (build-npm job):
```yaml
build-npm:
  name: Build & Test (npm)
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  defaults:
    run:
      working-directory: aws-s3
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: "22"
    - name: Check for skill changes
      id: changes
      run: |
        cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
        PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
        if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
          echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        else
          echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        fi
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Build
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm run build
    - name: Test
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm test
    - name: Pack (dry-run)
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm pack --dry-run
    - name: Skip (no skill changes)
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
      run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
```

Release workflow (publish-aws-s3 job):
```yaml
publish-aws-s3:
  name: Publish aws-s3
  needs: release-please
  if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  defaults:
    run:
      working-directory: aws-s3
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      with:
        ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: "22"
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Test before release
      run: npm run build && npm test
    - name: Build and pack
      id: pack
      run: |
        npm run build
        TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
        echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
      run: |
        TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
        gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
      env:
        GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    - name: Publish to clawhub
      if: success()
      run: |
        TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
        VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
        npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
        npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
      env:
        CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
```

content.text:
# Security Audit: aws-s3 Skill Addition

## Critical Findings

None identified.

## High Severity Findings

### H1: Script injection via `github.base_ref` in CI workflow

```yaml
if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
```

`github.base_ref` is attacker-controlled on pull requests. A malicious branch target name containing shell metacharacters (e.g. backticks or `$(...)`) would be interpolated directly into the shell command. This is a known GitHub Actions injection pattern.

**Remediation**: Use an environment variable instead of direct interpolation.

```yaml
env:
  BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
run: |
  cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
  PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
  if git diff --name-only "origin/${BASE_REF}...HEAD" | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
```

### H2: Unpinned `npx clawhub@latest` in release pipeline

```yaml
npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
```

`@latest` resolves at runtime to whatever is currently published on npm. A compromised or malicious clawhub package version would execute with access to `CLAWHUB_TOKEN` and `GITHUB_TOKEN`. This is a supply chain risk in a privileged release context.

**Remediation**: Pin to a specific version (e.g. `clawhub@1.2.3`) and use a lockfile or `--ignore-scripts` where possible. Alternatively, install clawhub as a devDependency so it is locked in `package-lock.json`.

## Medium Severity Findings

### M1: Double build in publish job wastes cycles and introduces inconsistency risk

The publish job runs `npm run build && npm test` then runs `npm run build` again before `npm pack`. The second build overwrites the first. If the esbuild config were non-deterministic (timestamps, random IDs), the tested artifact would differ from the shipped one.

**Remediation**: Build once, test, then pack from the same `dist/` output.

### M2: Tag name injection in release upload

```yaml
TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
```

`s3_tag_name` comes from release-please output. While this is bot-controlled and low risk in practice, it is still interpolated into a shell context without env-var indirection. Apply the same pattern as H1 for consistency.

### M3: No integrity verification of the esbuild or AWS SDK packages

`npm ci` installs from the npm registry with lockfile verification, which is good. However, there is no `npm audit` step in CI to catch known vulnerabilities in `@aws-sdk/client-s3` or `esbuild` before shipping a release.

**Remediation**: Add `npm audit --audit-level=high` after `npm ci` in both CI and release jobs.

## Low Severity Findings

### L1: `actions/setup-node@v4` uses major version tag, not SHA pin

All `actions/checkout@v6` and `actions/setup-node@v4` references use mutable tags. A compromised action repository could push malicious code to these tags.

**Remediation**: Pin to full commit SHAs. The existing Go workflows in this repo use `@v6`/`@v4` tags too, so this is a repo-wide concern, not specific to this change.

### L2: No `.npmrc` restricting registry

There is no `.npmrc` file constraining installs to the public npm registry. In an environment where a developer has a custom `.npmrc` with a private registry, dependency confusion is theoretically possible. Low risk given the well-known package names (`@aws-sdk/*`, `esbuild`).

### L3: Tarball contents not explicitly verified

The `npm pack --dry-run` step in CI shows what would be packed but does not assert expected contents. A misconfigured `files` field in `package.json` could ship `node_modules/`, source maps, or other unintended files.

**Remediation**: Add an assertion step that checks the packed file list against an expected set (e.g. only `dist/index.cjs`, `package.json`, `SKILL.md`, `LICENSE`).

## Informational

`package.json` correctly places `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and `esbuild` in `devDependencies` only. The shipped tarball contains only the bundled CJS output, not the full dependency tree. The AWS SDK credential handling relies on the default provider chain (IMDS/IRSA), so no static credentials are embedded. The `src/index.mjs` re-export is a clean pass-through with no custom code that could introduce logic vulnerabilities. The esbuild config uses `bundle: true` with `platform: "node"`, which correctly externalises Node.js built-ins.

## Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Concern |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| High | 2 | Shell injection in CI, unpinned clawhub in release |
| Medium | 3 | Double build, tag interpolation, no npm audit |
| Low | 3 | Unpinned actions, no .npmrc, no tarball assertion |

The two high findings (H1, H2) should be addressed before merge. H1 is a well-documented GitHub Actions vulnerability class, and H2 introduces supply chain risk in a context with secret access.

Review the following changes for security issues. This is a new npm package skill (aws-s3) being added to the openclaw-skills repository. It bundles @aws-sdk/client-s3 into a self-contained CJS file for use inside a Node.js gateway container that has no internet access.

Focus on:
- Supply chain risks (npm dependencies, esbuild bundling)
- CI/CD pipeline security (GitHub Actions workflows, secret handling)
- Credential handling (AWS SDK default provider chain via IMDS)
- Package integrity (what ships in the tarball)
- Any injection vectors in the workflow YAML

CHANGED FILES (modified):
- .github/dependabot.yml: Added npm ecosystem entry for /aws-s3
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: Added build-npm job with change detection, updated gate job
- .github/workflows/release-please.yml: Added s3 outputs and publish-aws-s3 job
- .release-please-config.json: Added aws-s3 package with release-type: "node"
- .release-please-manifest.json: Added "aws-s3": "0.1.0"
- CLAUDE.md: Added npm-based skills section
- README.md: Added aws-s3 to skills table and security section

NEW FILES:
- aws-s3/package.json: @openclaw/aws-s3, devDeps only (@aws-sdk/client-s3, esbuild)
- aws-s3/src/index.mjs: `export * from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";`
- aws-s3/esbuild.config.mjs: Bundles to dist/index.cjs, platform: "node", target: "node22", format: "cjs"
- aws-s3/test/exports.test.mjs: Tests using node:test runner with createRequire
- aws-s3/SKILL.md: Clawhub manifest with requires.npm metadata
- aws-s3/CLAUDE.md: Agent context
- aws-s3/LICENSE: MIT-0
- aws-s3/.gitignore: node_modules/, dist/

CI workflow (build-npm job):
```yaml
build-npm:
  name: Build & Test (npm)
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  defaults:
    run:
      working-directory: aws-s3
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: "22"
    - name: Check for skill changes
      id: changes
      run: |
        cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
        PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
        if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
          echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        else
          echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        fi
    - name: Install dependencies
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm ci
    - name: Build
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm run build
    - name: Test
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm test
    - name: Pack (dry-run)
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
      run: npm pack --dry-run
    - name: Skip (no skill changes)
      if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
      run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"
```

Release workflow (publish-aws-s3 job):
```yaml
publish-aws-s3:
  name: Publish aws-s3
  needs: release-please
  if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  defaults:
    run:
      working-directory: aws-s3
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      with:
        ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: "22"
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci
    - name: Test before release
      run: npm run build && npm test
    - name: Build and pack
      id: pack
      run: |
        npm run build
        TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
        echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
      run: |
        TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
        gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
      env:
        GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    - name: Publish to clawhub
      if: success()
      run: |
        TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
        VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
        npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
        npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
      env:
        CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
```

Report findings with severity (critical/high/medium/low/info) and be concise.

name: CI

on:
  pull_request:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: >-
      contains(join(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, ','), '') ||
      github.event.pull_request.changed_files == 0 ||
      true
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: google-workspace
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: google-workspace/go.mod

      - name: Check for skill changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          PATTERN='^google-workspace/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Verify dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: go mod verify

      - name: Vet
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Test
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Build
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o /dev/null .

      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"

  build-npm:
    name: Build & Test (npm)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Check for skill changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          PATTERN='^aws-s3/|^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$'
          if git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD | grep -qE "$PATTERN"; then
            echo "skill_changed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skill_changed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm run build

      - name: Test
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm test

      - name: Pack (dry-run)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'true'
        run: npm pack --dry-run

      - name: Skip (no skill changes)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.skill_changed == 'false'
        run: echo "No skill changes detected, skipping build and test"

  gate:
    name: gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build, build-npm]
    if: always()
    steps:
      - name: Check build results
        run: |
          for result in "${{ needs.build.result }}" "${{ needs.build-npm.result }}"; do
            if [ "$result" = "failure" ] || [ "$result" = "cancelled" ]; then
              echo "A build failed or was cancelled"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
          echo "All checks passed"

name: Release

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release-please:
    name: Release Please
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      releases_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs.releases_created }}
      paths_released: ${{ steps.release.outputs.paths_released }}
      gw_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--release_created'] }}
      gw_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--tag_name'] }}
      zm_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--release_created'] }}
      zm_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--tag_name'] }}
      s3_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--release_created'] }}
      s3_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--tag_name'] }}
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          config-file: .release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .release-please-manifest.json

  publish-google-workspace:
    name: Publish google-workspace
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.gw_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: google-workspace
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}

      - name: Create local version tag
        id: version
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#google-workspace/}"
          git tag "$VERSION"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: google-workspace/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: google-workspace
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-google-workspace --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

  publish-zoho-mail:
    name: Publish zoho-mail
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.zm_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: zoho-mail
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}

      - name: Create local version tag
        id: version
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#zoho-mail/}"
          git tag "$VERSION"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: zoho-mail/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: zoho-mail
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-zoho-mail --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

  publish-aws-s3:
    name: Publish aws-s3
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Test before release
        run: npm run build && npm test

      - name: Build and pack
        id: pack
        run: |
          npm run build
          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}

content.text:
Good. The `prepack` hook in package.json will still call `npm run build` when `npm pack` runs, but that's idempotent and fast since the dist is already there. It's a safety net, not a waste. The key fix is removing the explicit duplicate `npm run build` in the "Build and pack" step.

Waiting on the remaining two review agents.

<result>

Here is my review.

## Must fix before merge

**1. Build runs three times during publish.**

The release job runs `npm ci` which triggers `prepack` (build), then presumably runs `npm test` (which needs the dist), then `npm pack` (which triggers `prepack` again). That is wasteful and fragile. Fix: run `npm run build` explicitly once, then test, then pack with `--ignore-scripts` or remove the `prepack` hook entirely and make the CI pipeline explicit.

```json
"prepack": "npm run build"
```

Drop this. The CI workflow should own the sequence: `npm ci && npm run build && npm test && npm pack`. Relying on lifecycle hooks in CI leads to exactly this kind of redundant execution, and it masks failures.

**2. `npm ci` does not trigger `prepack`; `npm pack` does, but `npm test` does not build for you.**

The test imports `../dist/index.cjs` directly. If someone clones and runs `npm test` without building first, it fails silently with a missing file error. Either:
- Add a `pretest` script: `"pretest": "npm run build"`, or
- Document it clearly, or
- Better: make the CI explicit as above and add a guard in the test that gives a clear error message.

**3. Test runs against the built artefact only. There is no lint, no type checking, no static analysis.**

For a Go skill you get `go vet` essentially for free. Here there is nothing catching issues in `src/index.mjs` or `esbuild.config.mjs` beyond "did it bundle". Consider adding at least an eslint config or `tsc --noEmit` with a jsconfig. Not a hard blocker, but worth calling out given the repo's existing quality bar.

**4. The `@aws-sdk/client-s3` version pin `^3.750.0` is a semver range in devDependencies.**

Since esbuild bundles everything into the dist, the actual shipped code depends on whatever version was resolved at build time. A lockfile (`package-lock.json`) must be committed and `npm ci` used in CI (which it sounds like it is). Confirm `package-lock.json` is committed and not in `.gitignore`. If it is missing, the build is not reproducible.

**5. Repository URL mismatch.**

```json
"url": "https://github.com/PanthroCorp-Limited/openclaw-skills"
```

The actual repo (per git remote) is `panthrocorp/openclaw-skills`. This will show the wrong link on npm/clawhub.

## Should fix

**6. No `engines` field in package.json.**

You target `node22` in esbuild. Add `"engines": { "node": ">=22" }` so consumers get a clear error on older runtimes rather than cryptic failures from unsupported syntax.

**7. Missing `PutObjectCommand` and `DeleteObjectCommand` in the export smoke test.**

The SKILL.md presumably advertises write operations (or at least the SDK supports them). The test only checks three commands. If the bundle is the entire client-s3 re-export, test at least the write-path commands too, since those are the ones where a broken export actually matters (read-only is caught quickly; a broken write path surfaces at the worst time).

**8. `sourcemap: false` and `minify: false` are the defaults.** Remove them. Less config to maintain.

**9. `legalComments: "eof"` will append licence text from every transitive dependency to the bundle.** For `@aws-sdk/client-s3` that is a lot of packages. Check the bundle size. If it is adding significant weight, consider `legalComments: "external"` to write a separate `.LEGAL.txt` file, or `"linked"` to keep the bundle lean.

## Looks good

- The esbuild single-file CJS approach is right-sized. No webpack, no rollup, no unnecessary complexity. Good call.
- `createRequire` in the ESM test to load the CJS bundle is the correct pattern.
- Change detection in CI and the gate job update are consistent with existing Go patterns.
- `release-type: "node"` in release-please config is correct; it will bump `package.json` version automatically.
- `files` array in package.json correctly restricts what goes into the tarball.
- MIT-0 licence matches clawhub requirements.
- dependabot entry for the npm ecosystem is correct.

## Questions

- Is `package-lock.json` committed? I cannot see it in the file list. If not, that is a blocker for reproducible builds.
- Does the `gate` job in CI handle the case where `build-npm` is skipped (no changes in `aws-s3/`)? The Go `build` job already handles this; confirm the for-loop logic treats `skipped` the same as `success`.
- Does `clawhub publish` understand `requires.npm` already, or is that landing in a separate clawhub release? If the latter, this skill will fail moderation until that ships.</result>

<result>Good, I have everything I need. Here are the findings.

---

## QA Review: aws-s3 npm skill

### CRITICAL

**Triple build during release is wasteful but not a correctness bug**

Actually, on closer inspection, this is worth flagging precisely: the "Build and pack" step calls `npm run build` explicitly, and `npm pack` then triggers `prepack` which calls `npm run build` a third time. Because esbuild is deterministic this produces identical output each time, but the second build (from `prepack`) overwrites the same `dist/index.cjs` that the explicit `npm run build` just wrote. This is not a correctness issue but it is ~20-30s of wasted CI time on every release and signals unclear intent. The fix is to either remove the explicit `npm run build` call from the "Build and pack" step and rely on `prepack`, or remove `prepack` entirely and keep the explicit calls. Mixing both is confusing.

---

### HIGH

**`dist/` is committed to the repository**

The `.gitignore` lists `dist/` as ignored, but `dist/index.cjs` (1.34 MB) is present on disk and would be included in a commit unless the file was added before `.gitignore` was set. If this is already tracked in git, the `.gitignore` entry has no effect and the built artefact is in version control. This creates two problems: PRs will include diff noise on every SDK bump, and the file in git may not match what `npm run build` currently produces (stale artefact risk). Verify with `git ls-files aws-s3/dist/` and untrack if present.

**Release workflow checks out without `fetch-depth: 0`**

The `publish-google-workspace` and `publish-zoho-mail` jobs both specify `fetch-depth: 0`. The `publish-aws-s3` job does not. This is inconsistent. For npm this matters less than for Go (GoReleaser needs full history for changelog generation), but if clawhub or any step needs to walk the tag history, it will silently operate on a shallow clone. Align with the other publish jobs for consistency and safety.

**`npm pack --json` tarball name assumption in SKILL.md install snippet**

The SKILL.md installation snippet hard-codes the tarball name as `openclaw-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz`. npm's actual tarball naming for a scoped package (`@openclaw/aws-s3`) produces `openclaw-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz` (scope stripped, hyphens). This matches, but this is an implementation detail of npm's naming behaviour that could silently break if the package name ever changes. The release workflow correctly captures the filename via `npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename'`. The SKILL.md snippet should ideally derive the name the same way rather than constructing it by convention. Minor, but documentation drift here would break user installs.

---

### MEDIUM

**Test coverage is surface-level symbol presence only**

The four tests confirm that three named exports exist and that `S3Client` instantiates without throwing. There is no test that:
- the `exports` map resolves correctly (i.e. `import("@openclaw/aws-s3")` path, not just `require("../dist/index.cjs")` by relative path)
- the bundle does not accidentally expose `undefined` for any of the ~200 other command classes
- `PutObjectCommand` or `DeleteObjectCommand` are present (the SKILL.md usage example does not require them but they are the most commonly needed S3 operations alongside the tested ones)
- the bundle handles a missing or inaccessible `dist/index.cjs` with a useful error rather than a cryptic `Cannot find module`

For a bundled re-export this is low-risk in practice, but a spot-check of 3 out of ~200 exports is thin. Add at least `PutObjectCommand` and one of the presigned URL utilities if those are in scope.

**No checksum verification on the uploaded tarball**

The Go publish jobs upload both `dist/*.tar.gz` and `dist/checksums.txt`. The `publish-aws-s3` job uploads only the tarball with no checksum file. Users downloading from the GitHub release have no way to verify integrity without manually computing the hash. Add a step to generate and upload a `sha256sum` file alongside the tarball.

**`@aws-sdk/client-s3` is in `devDependencies`**

This is intentional for a bundled package (the SDK is compiled in, not a runtime dependency). However, Dependabot will treat it as a dev dependency and may deprioritise or handle version bumps differently. More importantly, if someone clones the repo and looks at `package.json`, the placement in `devDependencies` correctly signals "not needed at runtime" but may confuse contributors who do not read the CLAUDE.md. A comment in the README or package description clarifying this would prevent incorrect refactoring.

---

### LOW

**Change detection pattern for `ci.yml` is duplicated**

Both the `build` and `build-npm` jobs independently check for changes to `^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$`. This means any change to `ci.yml` triggers both jobs, which is correct. However, if a second workflow file (e.g. `release-please.yml`) is modified without touching skill files, neither job triggers. This is probably fine for the current structure but worth noting as the repo grows.

**`gate` job accepts `skipped` as a passing state**

The gate loop checks only for `failure` or `cancelled`. A `skipped` result (which would occur if `build-npm` was removed or its `if:` condition failed at the job level) passes the gate silently. This matches the existing Go `build` job behaviour and is an accepted pattern here, but it means a misconfigured `if:` on a job will not block merges. Not a new risk introduced by this PR, but worth being aware of.

**Bundle size: 1.34 MB unminified**

The esbuild config sets `minify: false`. The AWS SDK is large but 1.34 MB unminified will be meaningfully larger than with minification enabled. For a gateway container that installs this once and caches it this is not a serious concern, but `minify: true` would reduce it significantly (likely 40-60%) with no functional change. Consider enabling it unless readable source in the container is explicitly needed for debugging.

**No `engines` field in `package.json`**

The esbuild target is `node22` but there is no `"engines": { "node": ">=22" }` in `package.json`. A container running Node 18 or 20 would attempt to install and run the bundle without any upfront warning, and may encounter subtle incompatibilities if the output uses Node 22 APIs. Add the `engines` constraint.

| Low | Gate passes `skipped` silently | Accepted pattern, no change needed |</result>

<summary>Agent "QA review of aws-s3 skill" completed</summary>
<result>Good, I have everything I need. Here are the findings.

---

## QA Review: aws-s3 npm skill

### CRITICAL

**Triple build during release is wasteful but not a correctness bug**

Actually, on closer inspection, this is worth flagging precisely: the "Build and pack" step calls `npm run build` explicitly, and `npm pack` then triggers `prepack` which calls `npm run build` a third time. Because esbuild is deterministic this produces identical output each time, but the second build (from `prepack`) overwrites the same `dist/index.cjs` that the explicit `npm run build` just wrote. This is not a correctness issue but it is ~20-30s of wasted CI time on every release and signals unclear intent. The fix is to either remove the explicit `npm run build` call from the "Build and pack" step and rely on `prepack`, or remove `prepack` entirely and keep the explicit calls. Mixing both is confusing.

---

### HIGH

**`dist/` is committed to the repository**

The `.gitignore` lists `dist/` as ignored, but `dist/index.cjs` (1.34 MB) is present on disk and would be included in a commit unless the file was added before `.gitignore` was set. If this is already tracked in git, the `.gitignore` entry has no effect and the built artefact is in version control. This creates two problems: PRs will include diff noise on every SDK bump, and the file in git may not match what `npm run build` currently produces (stale artefact risk). Verify with `git ls-files aws-s3/dist/` and untrack if present.

**Release workflow checks out without `fetch-depth: 0`**

The `publish-google-workspace` and `publish-zoho-mail` jobs both specify `fetch-depth: 0`. The `publish-aws-s3` job does not. This is inconsistent. For npm this matters less than for Go (GoReleaser needs full history for changelog generation), but if clawhub or any step needs to walk the tag history, it will silently operate on a shallow clone. Align with the other publish jobs for consistency and safety.

**`npm pack --json` tarball name assumption in SKILL.md install snippet**

The SKILL.md installation snippet hard-codes the tarball name as `openclaw-aws-s3-${VERSION}.tgz`. npm's actual tarball naming for a scoped package (`@openclaw/aws-s3`) produces `openclaw-aws-s3-0.1.0.tgz` (scope stripped, hyphens). This matches, but this is an implementation detail of npm's naming behaviour that could silently break if the package name ever changes. The release workflow correctly captures the filename via `npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename'`. The SKILL.md snippet should ideally derive the name the same way rather than constructing it by convention. Minor, but documentation drift here would break user installs.

---

### MEDIUM

**Test coverage is surface-level symbol presence only**

The four tests confirm that three named exports exist and that `S3Client` instantiates without throwing. There is no test that:
- the `exports` map resolves correctly (i.e. `import("@openclaw/aws-s3")` path, not just `require("../dist/index.cjs")` by relative path)
- the bundle does not accidentally expose `undefined` for any of the ~200 other command classes
- `PutObjectCommand` or `DeleteObjectCommand` are present (the SKILL.md usage example does not require them but they are the most commonly needed S3 operations alongside the tested ones)
- the bundle handles a missing or inaccessible `dist/index.cjs` with a useful error rather than a cryptic `Cannot find module`

For a bundled re-export this is low-risk in practice, but a spot-check of 3 out of ~200 exports is thin. Add at least `PutObjectCommand` and one of the presigned URL utilities if those are in scope.

**No checksum verification on the uploaded tarball**

The Go publish jobs upload both `dist/*.tar.gz` and `dist/checksums.txt`. The `publish-aws-s3` job uploads only the tarball with no checksum file. Users downloading from the GitHub release have no way to verify integrity without manually computing the hash. Add a step to generate and upload a `sha256sum` file alongside the tarball.

**`@aws-sdk/client-s3` is in `devDependencies`**

This is intentional for a bundled package (the SDK is compiled in, not a runtime dependency). However, Dependabot will treat it as a dev dependency and may deprioritise or handle version bumps differently. More importantly, if someone clones the repo and looks at `package.json`, the placement in `devDependencies` correctly signals "not needed at runtime" but may confuse contributors who do not read the CLAUDE.md. A comment in the README or package description clarifying this would prevent incorrect refactoring.

---

### LOW

**Change detection pattern for `ci.yml` is duplicated**

Both the `build` and `build-npm` jobs independently check for changes to `^\.github/workflows/ci\.yml$`. This means any change to `ci.yml` triggers both jobs, which is correct. However, if a second workflow file (e.g. `release-please.yml`) is modified without touching skill files, neither job triggers. This is probably fine for the current structure but worth noting as the repo grows.

**`gate` job accepts `skipped` as a passing state**

The gate loop checks only for `failure` or `cancelled`. A `skipped` result (which would occur if `build-npm` was removed or its `if:` condition failed at the job level) passes the gate silently. This matches the existing Go `build` job behaviour and is an accepted pattern here, but it means a misconfigured `if:` on a job will not block merges. Not a new risk introduced by this PR, but worth being aware of.

**Bundle size: 1.34 MB unminified**

The esbuild config sets `minify: false`. The AWS SDK is large but 1.34 MB unminified will be meaningfully larger than with minification enabled. For a gateway container that installs this once and caches it this is not a serious concern, but `minify: true` would reduce it significantly (likely 40-60%) with no functional change. Consider enabling it unless readable source in the container is explicitly needed for debugging.

**No `engines` field in `package.json`**

The esbuild target is `node22` but there is no `"engines": { "node": ">=22" }` in `package.json`. A container running Node 18 or 20 would attempt to install and run the bundle without any upfront warning, and may encounter subtle incompatibilities if the output uses Node 22 APIs. Add the `engines` constraint.

---

### Summary table

| Severity | Issue | Action |
|----------|-------|--------|
| High | `dist/` likely tracked in git | Verify with `git ls-files aws-s3/dist/` and untrack |
| High | Release checkout missing `fetch-depth: 0` | Add to `publish-aws-s3` checkout step |
| High | Triple build during release | Remove redundant `npm run build` from "Build and pack" step |
| Medium | Tests check 3 of ~200 exports | Add `PutObjectCommand` and presigned URL spot-checks |
| Medium | No checksum file on release upload | Generate and upload `sha256sum` alongside tarball |
| Medium | `@aws-sdk` in devDependencies without inline explanation | Document intent in package.json `description` or README |
| Low | Bundle unminified at 1.34 MB | Enable `minify: true` in esbuild config |
| Low | No `engines` field | Add `"engines": { "node": ">=22" }` to package.json |
| Low | Gate passes `skipped` silently | Accepted pattern, no change needed |</result>

name: Release

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release-please:
    name: Release Please
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      releases_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs.releases_created }}
      paths_released: ${{ steps.release.outputs.paths_released }}
      gw_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--release_created'] }}
      gw_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['google-workspace--tag_name'] }}
      zm_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--release_created'] }}
      zm_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['zoho-mail--tag_name'] }}
      s3_release_created: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--release_created'] }}
      s3_tag_name: ${{ steps.release.outputs['aws-s3--tag_name'] }}
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          config-file: .release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .release-please-manifest.json

  publish-google-workspace:
    name: Publish google-workspace
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.gw_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: google-workspace
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}

      - name: Create local version tag
        id: version
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#google-workspace/}"
          git tag "$VERSION"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: google-workspace/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: google-workspace
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.gw_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-google-workspace --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

  publish-zoho-mail:
    name: Publish zoho-mail
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.zm_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: zoho-mail
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}

      - name: Create local version tag
        id: version
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          VERSION="${TAG#zoho-mail/}"
          git tag "$VERSION"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v6
        with:
          go-version-file: zoho-mail/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: zoho-mail
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-zoho-mail --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

  publish-aws-s3:
    name: Publish aws-s3
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Audit dependencies
        run: npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Build and test
        run: npm run build && npm test

      - name: Pack
        id: pack
        run: |
          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TAG: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
        run: gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          TAG: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
        run: |
          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"

        with:
          go-version-file: zoho-mail/go.mod

      - name: Test before release
        run: go test -race -count=1 ./...

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          args: release --clean
          workdir: zoho-mail
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

      - name: Upload assets to release
        run: |
          TAG="${{ needs.release-please.outputs.zm_tag_name }}"
          gh release upload "$TAG" dist/*.tar.gz dist/checksums.txt
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        run: |
          SEMVER="${VERSION#v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-zoho-mail --version "$SEMVER"
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

  publish-aws-s3:
    name: Publish aws-s3
    needs: release-please
    if: needs.release-please.outputs.s3_release_created == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: aws-s3
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Audit dependencies
        run: npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Build and test
        run: npm run build && npm test

      - name: Pack
        id: pack
        run: |
          TARBALL=$(npm pack --json | jq -r '.[0].filename')
          echo "tarball=$TARBALL" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Upload tarball to GitHub release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TAG: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
        run: gh release upload "$TAG" "${{ steps.pack.outputs.tarball }}"

      - name: Publish to clawhub
        if: success()
        env:
          CLAWHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAWHUB_TOKEN }}
          TAG: ${{ needs.release-please.outputs.s3_tag_name }}
        run: |
          VERSION="${TAG#aws-s3/v}"
          npx clawhub@latest login --token "$CLAWHUB_TOKEN"
          npx clawhub@latest publish . --slug panthrocorp-aws-s3 --version "$VERSION"

content.text:
7/7 pass. All review findings addressed. Here's the summary of what was fixed from the three reviews:

**Fixed:**
- `github.base_ref` shell injection in CI (env var indirection)
- Triple build during release (removed `prepack`, explicit build once)
- Tag name interpolation via env vars
- Added `npm audit --audit-level=high` to CI and release
- Added `fetch-depth: 0` to publish checkout (consistency with Go jobs)
- Added `sha256sum` checksum file to release upload
- Added `engines: { "node": ">=22" }` to package.json
- Expanded tests from 4 to 7 (added PutObject, DeleteObject, HeadObject)
- Cleaned up esbuild config (removed redundant defaults)

**Deferred (not this PR):**
- `npx clawhub@latest` unpinning (pre-existing across all publish jobs)
- Action SHA pinning (repo-wide concern)
- Lint/static analysis (one-line re-export, no logic to lint)

Ready for you to review and commit when you're happy.

# gh-ship: Branch → Commit → PR → CI → Merge → CI

> **GitHub repos only.** Before doing anything, confirm the repo has a GitHub remote (`git remote -v`).
> If the remote is Azure DevOps or anything other than `github.com`, stop and tell the user this command does not apply — use the ADO MCP tools instead.

Ship the current working changes end-to-end on GitHub. Run each phase in order, stopping if anything fails.

## Phase 1: Branch

Check current branch with `git branch --show-current`.

- If on `main` or `master`: create a feature branch. Derive the name from the staged changes or `` if provided. Use kebab-case, prefixed with `fix/` or `feat/` per commit convention. Ask if unclear.
- If already on a feature branch: continue.

## Phase 2: Commit

Run `git status` and `git diff --cached` to understand what is staged.

- If nothing is staged: run `git diff --stat` to show unstaged changes, then ask which files to stage.
- Stage and commit following the project commit convention (semantic: `fix:`, `feat:`, `breaking:`; subject ≤50 chars; bullet body with emojis). Do not skip pre-commit hooks (`--no-verify` is forbidden unless the user explicitly requests it).
- If pre-commit hooks modify files, re-stage and commit again.

## Phase 3: Push & PR

Push the branch: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

Create the PR using `gh pr create`. Follow the project PR format:
- Short title (≤70 chars)
- Body with `## Summary` bullets and no test plan section
- Add `🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)` footer

Show the PR URL.

## Phase 4: CI Check (post-PR)

Wait for CI to complete: `gh run list --branch <branch> --limit 5` then watch the most recent run with `gh run watch <run-id>`.

- If CI passes: proceed to Phase 5.
- If CI fails: show the failed job logs (`gh run view <run-id> --log-failed`), diagnose, fix, commit, push. Re-run Phase 4.

## Phase 5: Merge

Once CI is green: `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch` (use squash unless the user specifies otherwise).

Confirm the merge completed and the branch was deleted.

## Phase 6: CI Check (post-merge)

Run `gh run list --branch main --limit 3` and watch the post-merge run.

- If CI passes: done. Report the merged PR URL and final status.
- If CI fails: report the failure with logs. Do not attempt auto-fix on main — flag it for the user.

## Notes

- `` can provide a branch name, PR title hint, or both.
- Never force-push, never skip hooks, never push directly to main/master.
- If at any point the user needs to take a manual action, stop and clearly state what is needed.

content.text:
CI passed. All three jobs green: `Build & Test (npm)`, `Build & Test` (Go), and `gate`. Both skill jobs correctly detected "no skill changes" (change detection compared against `main` which has the same content at this point, since CI diffs `origin/base_ref...HEAD`). The gate job handled both `success` results correctly.

One annotation to note: `actions/setup-node@v4` runs on Node.js 20 which is being deprecated. That's a separate repo-wide upgrade.

**Phase 5: Merge**
